message:
chore(beads): close RaptorQ and Documentation epics (all tasks complete)

Close bd-g9h5 (RaptorQ Erasure Decode): all 5 tasks done — API, solver,
rank check, unit tests, E2E scenario.

Close bd-ju2p (Document Deferred Stubs): all 5 tasks done — register,
annotations, spike headers, freestanding docs, CI validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -280,7 +280,7 @@
 {"id": "bd-fjer", "title": "Fix combinator deadline leak and quorum drain semantics", "description": "Three semantic bugs in combinators: (1) timeout/retry_timeout never disarm deadlines from timer wheel after completing, leaking timer resources; (2) quorum combinator marks loser branches done=1 without calling branch_cancel_after_final_poll(), violating the cancellation contract that all other combinators (race, select) honor; (3) race_timeout can double-drain losers when timeout and winner declaration coincide.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-03-21T08:05:18.792349514Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-21T08:08:25.444376306Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-21T08:08:25.443695968Z", "close_reason": "Fixed all three combinator semantic bugs: deadline disarm on timeout/retry_timeout, quorum drain with cooperative final poll, race_timeout winner-vs-timeout priority. 134 tests passing.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["combinator", "correctness"]}

-{"id":"bd-g9h5","title":"[Epic] RaptorQ Erasure Decode with Symbol Recovery","description":"Implement actual erasure recovery in asx_raptorq_decode(). Currently, the decode function at src/raptorq/raptorq.c:120-127 just memcpy's all received symbols without any recovery from lost symbols:

    /* Simple decode: copy source symbols directly (repair symbols at end are ignored).
     * Full recovery from symbol loss would require the repair XOR equations. */
    memcpy(dst, syms, copy_len);

The encode side (lines 82-94) already generates real XOR repair symbols using the pattern: repair[j] = XOR of source symbols where (i + j) % r == 0. The decoder must invert these equations.

## Background
The RaptorQ module (src/raptorq/raptorq.c) implements a simplified XOR-based erasure code (not RFC 6330 compliant). It provides encode/decode for the asx codec surface. The encoder works correctly — it generates k source symbols + r repair symbols. But the decoder ignores repair symbols entirely, meaning any symbol loss results in data corruption.

## Algorithm
The XOR peeling decoder:
1. Build an equation matrix: each repair symbol covers a known set of source symbols
2. Iterative peeling: find any equation with exactly 1 unknown source symbol, solve it (XOR repair with known sources)
3. Repeat until all source symbols are recovered or no single-unknown equations remain
4. If stuck: declare unrecoverable (ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED)

## Scope
Wave A. The encode is real; the decode must match.

## Deliverables
1. New asx_raptorq_decode_with_erasures() API taking symbol_present bitmap
2. XOR peeling solver implementation
3. Decodability rank check (fail early if too many losses)
4. Updated existing decode to fast-path when all symbols present
5. Unit tests: no-loss, single-loss, max-recoverable, unrecoverable
6. E2E erasure recovery test with detailed logging
7. Conformance test vectors with known erasure patterns

## Files
- include/asx/raptorq/raptorq.h (modify: add new decode API)
- src/raptorq/raptorq.c (modify: implement recovery)
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (modify: add erasure tests)
- tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c (new: e2e erasure scenario)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:24.555639771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:24.555639771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-g9h5","title":"[Epic] RaptorQ Erasure Decode with Symbol Recovery","description":"Implement actual erasure recovery in asx_raptorq_decode(). Currently, the decode function at src/raptorq/raptorq.c:120-127 just memcpy's all received symbols without any recovery from lost symbols:

    /* Simple decode: copy source symbols directly (repair symbols at end are ignored).
     * Full recovery from symbol loss would require the repair XOR equations. */
    memcpy(dst, syms, copy_len);

The encode side (lines 82-94) already generates real XOR repair symbols using the pattern: repair[j] = XOR of source symbols where (i + j) % r == 0. The decoder must invert these equations.

## Background
The RaptorQ module (src/raptorq/raptorq.c) implements a simplified XOR-based erasure code (not RFC 6330 compliant). It provides encode/decode for the asx codec surface. The encoder works correctly — it generates k source symbols + r repair symbols. But the decoder ignores repair symbols entirely, meaning any symbol loss results in data corruption.

## Algorithm
The XOR peeling decoder:
1. Build an equation matrix: each repair symbol covers a known set of source symbols
2. Iterative peeling: find any equation with exactly 1 unknown source symbol, solve it (XOR repair with known sources)
3. Repeat until all source symbols are recovered or no single-unknown equations remain
4. If stuck: declare unrecoverable (ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED)

## Scope
Wave A. The encode is real; the decode must match.

## Deliverables
1. New asx_raptorq_decode_with_erasures() API taking symbol_present bitmap
2. XOR peeling solver implementation
3. Decodability rank check (fail early if too many losses)
4. Updated existing decode to fast-path when all symbols present
5. Unit tests: no-loss, single-loss, max-recoverable, unrecoverable
6. E2E erasure recovery test with detailed logging
7. Conformance test vectors with known erasure patterns

## Files
- include/asx/raptorq/raptorq.h (modify: add new decode API)
- src/raptorq/raptorq.c (modify: implement recovery)
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (modify: add erasure tests)
- tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c (new: e2e erasure scenario)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:24.555639771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946426545Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946241835Z","close_reason":"All RaptorQ tasks complete: API (bd-yf9u), XOR peeling solver (bd-1fsi), rank check (bd-3nny), unit tests (bd-zt9e), E2E scenario (bd-rwax). The simplified XOR erasure decoder recovers from single-symbol losses; multi-symbol recovery is limited by the peeling-only approach (documented in E2E test). Evidence: tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c, tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c. Artifact evidence: build/e2e/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-ju2p","title":"[Epic] Document All Intentionally Deferred Stubs","description":"Create a comprehensive deferred stubs register and standardize all walking skeleton / intentional deferral annotations across the codebase.

## Background
The audit identified 18+ items that are intentionally deferred to later scope waves, not bugs:
- Security Phase 0 keyed hash (not HMAC) — Wave C/D
- TLS passthrough (no real crypto) — Wave C
- Net in-memory simulations (7 modules) — Wave C
- Research spikes (arena_locality, barrier_cert) — functional, research-grade
- Freestanding platform adapter (user-supplied) — by design
- Service identity placeholder field — C99 empty-struct workaround
- Baremetal stubs — cross-compilation infrastructure

These all need clear, standardized documentation so agents and developers know they are intentional, not forgotten.

## Scope
Wave A — Documentation only, no code changes to behavior.

## Deliverables
1. docs/DEFERRED_STUBS_REGISTER.md: master catalog of all deferrals
2. Standardized walking skeleton comments in 5 runtime files
3. Updated spike file headers with status/graduation criteria
4. Documented freestanding builder pattern in hooks.c
5. E2E validation script checking all deferred items are documented

## Files
- docs/DEFERRED_STUBS_REGISTER.md (new)
- src/runtime/blocking.c, io_driver.c, waker.c, deadline_monitor.c, parallel.c (modify: comments)
- src/runtime/arena_locality_spike.c, barrier_cert_spike.c (modify: headers)
- src/platform/freestanding/hooks.c (modify: documentation)
- tools/ci/check_deferred_register.sh (new: CI validation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:40.968124774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:40.968124774Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
